senses.definition: Opposed to nature or to what is considered normal and natural.
examples: Some people see living in huge concrete cities as antinatural.
The writer criticized modern diets as antinatural and unhealthy.

senses.definition: The belief or attitude that rejects or opposes what is considered natural, often in philosophy or art.
examples: The philosopher’s antinaturalism led him to question the authority of nature.
In art history, antinaturalism appears in styles that avoid realistic representation.

senses.definition: A person who rejects or opposes naturalism, especially in philosophy, religion, or art.
examples: As an antinaturalist, she argued that moral values cannot be reduced to natural facts.
The movement’s leading antinaturalist wrote widely against realism in painting.

senses.definition: Opposing naturalism in style or theory, especially in art or philosophy.
examples: The artist took an antinaturalist approach, avoiding realistic detail.
His antinaturalist position rejects the idea that everything can be explained by science alone.

senses.definition: Not showing or supporting naturalism, especially by avoiding realistic representation or explanation.
examples: The director chose an antinaturalistic style, with symbolic sets and costumes.
Antinaturalistic ethics deny that moral rules can be based only on human nature.

senses.definition: In a way that is opposed to what is natural or normal.
examples: The trees had been antinaturally shaped into perfect cubes.
He spoke antinaturally slowly, as if reading each word from a script.

senses.definition: The quality of being opposed to nature or not normal and natural.
examples: Some critics pointed to the antinaturalness of the highly controlled environment.
The antinaturalness of the new law shocked many traditionalists.

senses.definition: Opposing or harmful to nature and the natural world.
examples: Some see intensive factory farming as fundamentally antinature.
The author warns against an antinature attitude that treats the planet as a machine.

senses.definition: Strong opposition to nature or to the value of the natural world.
examples: The critic accused modern industry of embracing antinature.
Religious antinature can appear in doctrines that deny value to the physical world.

senses.definition: Plural of antinature, used for different kinds or expressions of opposition to nature.
examples: The book compares various antinatures in modern culture, from virtual worlds to artificial food.
Different antinatures arise wherever technology replaces natural processes.

senses.definition: Used to prevent or reduce the feeling of wanting to vomit.
examples: She took an antinausea tablet before the long bus ride.
The doctor prescribed an antinausea drug for his chemotherapy treatment.

senses.definition: Showing prejudice or hostility against Black people; racist against Black people.
examples: The report documented a long history of antinegro policies in the region.
Antinegro attitudes were openly expressed in the newspaper at that time.

senses.definition: A person who is hostile or prejudiced against Black people.
examples: She refused to work with open antinegros in the organization.
The novel portrays an antinegro as the main antagonist.

senses.definition: Hostility, prejudice, or discrimination against Black people as a group.
examples: The study examines antinegroism in early twentieth-century law.
Antinegroism continues to shape many social institutions.

senses.definition: A person who strongly dislikes or opposes new words or expressions in a language.
examples: As an antineologian, he objected to almost every new slang term.
The antineologians in the academy resisted adding modern terms to the dictionary.

senses.definition: Acting to prevent, slow, or stop the growth of tumors or cancer cells.
examples: The patient received an antineoplastic drug as part of chemotherapy.
This antineoplastic treatment has shown good results in lung cancer.

senses.definition: A drug or substance that prevents or slows the growth of tumors or cancer cells.
examples: The doctor adjusted the dose of the antineoplastic to reduce side effects.
Common antineoplastics can damage both cancer cells and healthy cells.

senses.definition: Used to prevent or treat inflammation or disease of the kidneys.
examples: The antinephritic properties of the herb were noted in early medical texts.
She was given an antinephritic medicine to protect her kidneys.

senses.definition: A drug or substance used to prevent or treat kidney disease or inflammation.
examples: The doctor prescribed an antinephritic to reduce kidney inflammation.
Traditional healers used plant-based antinephritics.

senses.definition: Drugs or substances used to prevent or treat diseases of the kidneys.
examples: Modern antinephritics have fewer side effects than older drugs.
The textbook lists several antinephritics for acute nephritis.

senses.definition: Opposed to giving unfair advantages to relatives, especially in jobs or positions.
examples: The company adopted an antinepotic policy to avoid hiring family members.
Her antinepotic stance made her unpopular in a family-run firm.

senses.definition: Opposition to giving jobs or advantages to relatives and friends instead of choosing fairly.
examples: The new antinepotism rules require managers to declare family relationships.
Antinepotism is important for building trust in public institutions.

senses.definition: Different policies or forms of opposition to nepotism.
examples: Various antinepotisms have been introduced in different government agencies.
Corporate antinepotisms vary widely across countries.

senses.definition: Used to relieve or prevent nerve pain.
examples: The doctor prescribed an antineuralgic drug for her facial pain.
Some antineuralgic treatments can cause drowsiness.

senses.definition: A drug or substance that relieves nerve pain.
examples: He was given an antineuralgic to manage the sharp nerve pain.
Traditional antineuralgics included certain herbal extracts.

senses.definition: Used to prevent or treat neuritis, which is inflammation of a nerve.
examples: Antineuritic therapy can reduce inflammation and pain in damaged nerves.
Vitamin supplements may have antineuritic effects in some patients.

senses.definition: A drug or substance that treats or prevents neuritis.
examples: The doctor chose an antineuritic to treat the patient’s nerve inflammation.
Some antineuritics are given together with painkillers.

senses.definition: A substance that reduces or blocks the harmful effects of poisons on the nervous system.
examples: Researchers are testing a new antineurotoxin to counter snake venom.
Antineurotoxins may protect the brain from certain chemicals.

senses.definition: Opposed to a position of neutrality, especially in politics or war.
examples: Antineutral voices in the parliament pushed the country toward war.
The antineutral campaign argued that neutrality was morally wrong.

senses.definition: Opposition to a policy of neutrality, especially in international conflicts.
examples: Antineutralism grew stronger as the war spread.
The book explores antineutralism in early twentieth-century foreign policy.

senses.definition: The quality or policy of being against neutrality in conflicts or disputes.
examples: Public antineutrality forced the government to take sides.
Antineutrality was especially strong in the press during the crisis.

senses.definition: In a way that opposes neutrality.
examples: The newspaper wrote antineutrally, urging readers to support the war.
He spoke antineutrally about the conflict, demanding a clear choice of side.

senses.definition: A very light subatomic particle with no electric charge that is the antimatter partner of a neutrino.
examples: The experiment detected an antineutrino coming from the nuclear reactor.
Antineutrinos interact very weakly with matter, making them hard to observe.

senses.definition: Plural of antineutrino, referring to more than one such particle.
examples: Antineutrinos are produced in large numbers inside the sun.
Scientists measured the flow of antineutrinos from the reactor.

senses.definition: A subatomic particle that is the antimatter partner of a neutron and has the same mass but opposite properties.
examples: Physicists created an antineutron in a high-energy collision.
Antineutrons quickly annihilate when they meet ordinary matter.

senses.definition: Plural of antineutron, referring to more than one such particle.
examples: The collider produced a shower of antineutrons and other particles.
Antineutrons are studied to understand the properties of antimatter.

senses.definition: A behavior in which birds use ants, often rubbing them on their feathers, possibly to clean or protect themselves.
examples: The researcher observed a crow engaging in anting on the lawn.
Anting may help birds control parasites on their feathers.

senses.definition: In Filipino culture, a charm or amulet believed to give protection or special powers.
examples: He wore an antinganting around his neck for protection.
Stories about soldiers with powerful antinganting are common in local folklore.

senses.definition: Plural of anting, referring to several instances of the bird behavior of using ants on their feathers.
examples: The study recorded multiple antings over the summer.
Antings were most frequent after rainstorms, when ants were abundant.

senses.definition: Opposed to or rejecting denial; not supporting denial.
examples: Her antinial response showed she was ready to admit the problem.
The policy takes an antinial approach, encouraging open discussion rather than refusal.

senses.definition: Opposed to the use of nicotine or tobacco.
examples: The antinicotine campaign targeted young smokers.
Antinicotine messages were printed on all cigarette packs.

senses.definition: A substance used to reduce or block the effects of nicotine.
examples: Researchers are testing a new antinicotine to help people quit smoking.
The antinicotine reduced the rewarding feeling from cigarettes.

senses.definition: Opposition to nihilism, often defending the idea that life has meaning or values.
examples: His antinihilism is clear in his defence of moral duty.
Antinihilism became popular among thinkers who rejected despair.

senses.definition: A person who rejects nihilism and believes that life, values, or morals have real meaning.
examples: The antinihilist argued that human rights are objectively valid.
Many antinihilists stress the importance of hope and responsibility.

senses.definition: Rejecting nihilism and affirming that life or values have meaning.
examples: Her antinihilist philosophy offers a hopeful vision of human life.
The antinihilist message appealed to young readers.

senses.definition: Strongly opposing nihilism and supporting the idea that life and morals have real value.
examples: His antinihilistic writings defend the possibility of objective meaning.
The movement took an antinihilistic stand against despair.

senses.definition: A hypothetical particle proposed as the antimatter partner of an ion.
examples: The theory suggested the possible existence of an antinion.
Researchers discussed how an antinion might behave in a plasma.

senses.definition: Relating to or located at an antinode, where vibration in a wave is strongest.
examples: Maximum motion occurs at the antinodal points on the string.
The antinodal region showed the largest amplitude.

senses.definition: A point in a standing wave where the movement or amplitude is at its maximum.
examples: Each antinode on the string vibrates with the greatest amplitude.
Between two nodes there is one antinode.

senses.definition: Plural of antinode, referring to several points of maximum vibration in a standing wave.
examples: The string showed three clear antinodes during the experiment.
Antinodes occur halfway between the nodes.

senses.definition: Used to reduce or cancel unwanted sound, especially using sound waves with opposite phase.
examples: She bought antinoise headphones for the long flight.
Antinoise technology helps make car cabins quieter.

senses.definition: Plural of antinoise, referring to several systems or technologies that reduce unwanted sound.
examples: Modern cars use multiple antinoises to keep the cabin quiet.
Engineers compared different antinoises in their tests.

senses.definition: A principle or rule that conflicts with another equally reasonable principle or rule.
examples: The moral system contains an antinome that is hard to resolve.
Philosophers discussed the antinomes produced by strict rules.

senses.definition: Plural of antinome, referring to several conflicting but seemingly reasonable principles or rules.
examples: The legal system is full of antinomes that judges must balance.
The book explores antinomes between freedom and security.

senses.definition: A person who believes that moral or religious laws do not apply to people who have faith.
examples: The preacher denounced them as antinomians who rejected all rules.
Historically, antinomians were often accused of encouraging immorality.

senses.definition: Rejecting the binding force of moral or religious law.
examples: The movement was criticized for its antinomian teaching.
Antinomian ideas threatened the established church.

senses.definition: The belief that people of faith are released from the obligation to follow moral or religious laws.
examples: Antinomianism was condemned as heresy by many churches.
The book traces the history of antinomianism in Christian theology.

senses.definition: Different forms or instances of the belief that moral or religious laws do not bind believers.
examples: Scholars distinguish several antinomianisms in early Protestant movements.
Modern antinomianisms often appear in small sects.

senses.definition: Plural of antinomian, referring to people who reject the binding force of moral or religious law.
examples: The authorities accused the group of being antinomians.
Antinomians argued that grace freed them from legal rules.

senses.definition: Containing or showing a contradiction between two laws, principles, or conclusions that both seem reasonable.
examples: The theory leads to antinomic results that cannot all be true.
They tried to resolve the antinomic tension between freedom and order.
